docs.title: tv-scraper Documentation
docs.text:
tv-scraper helps you fetch TradingView data through a consistent Python API. The docs here focus on what to call, which inputs are accepted, what the response looks like, and where to find the exact valid values quickly.

Get Started Validation Reference

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
Use these pages when you want the fastest path to a working call:

  • Getting Started: install, first request, exports, cookies, and error checks.
  • API Basics: request patterns, response envelope, and one invalid-input example.
  • Reference Overview: jump straight to exchanges, timeframes, languages, news filters, and releases.
  • Releases & Versioning: see which docs versions exist and how the version switcher works.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import CandleStreamer

streamer = CandleStreamer()
result = streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    timeframe="1m",
    numb_candles=5,
    indicators=[("STD;RSI", "1.0")]
)

if result["status"] == "success":
    print(result["data"]["ohlcv"])
else:
    print(result["error"])

Every public method returns the same outer shape:

{
    "status": "success" | "failed",
    "data": ...,
    "metadata": {...},
    "warnings": [str, ...],
    "error": None | "message",
}

If a method asks for a specific exchange, timeframe, provider, language, or news filter, use the exact section links instead of scanning the whole docs site:

  • Exchanges

docs.title: Versions
docs.text: Versioned docs are designed around releases. Docs were introduced in v0.5.0, and the site is set up to use mike for release-aware navigation when published. See Releases & Versioning for the current version list and publishing policy.

docs.title: API Basics
docs.text: This page covers the request and response patterns that stay consistent across tv-scraper.

docs.title: Separate exchange and symbol
docs.text:
For symbol-based methods, pass them as two arguments:

scraper.get_technicals(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

Output structure:

{ "status": "success", "data": {"RSI": 54.21}, "metadata": { "exchange": "NASDAQ", "symbol": "AAPL", "timeframe": "1d", "technical_indicators": ["RSI"], }, "warnings": [], "error": None, }

docs.title: Use keyword arguments
docs.text:
Keyword arguments make the calls easier to read and reduce mistakes:

scraper.get_market_movers(
    market="stocks-usa",
    category="gainers",
    limit=10,
)

docs.title: Optional lists stay as Python lists
docs.text:
scraper.get_news(
    provider=["reuters", "tradingview"],
    market_country=["US", "IN"],
    market=["stock", "crypto"],
)

docs.title: Naming stays snake_case
docs.text: scraper.get_technicals(export="json")

docs.title: Response Envelope
docs.text:
Every public scraper method returns:

{ "status": "success" | "failed", "data": ..., "metadata": {...}, "warnings": [str, ...], "error": None | "message", }

docs.title: What changes by method
docs.text:
• data can be a dict, list, or None
  • metadata keeps the call context and method-specific details
  • warnings is a list of non-blocking messages (e.g. deprecations, indicator validation warnings)
  • error is None on success and a message on failure

docs.title: Important nuance
docs.text: Most failures return data=None, but some methods can return partial data with status="failed" when that is the intended behavior. The forecast streaming methods are the main example.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Error Handling

Public scraper methods return failures instead of raising data-access errors.

result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="INVALID",
    symbol="AAPL",
    technical_indicators=["RSI"],
)

{ "status": "failed", "data": None, "metadata": { "exchange": "INVALID", "symbol": "AAPL", "technical_indicators": ["RSI"], }, "warnings": [], "error": "Invalid value: 'INVALID'. ...", }

wrong input

This is the wrong style for public methods.

scraper.get_market_movers(market="stocks-usa", sortOrder="desc")

Use snake_case and correct parameters:

scraper.get_market_movers(market="stocks-usa", limit=20)

docs.title: Construction-Time Errors
docs.text:
Construction-Time Errors

Some constructor arguments are validated immediately and raise ValueError before any network activity:

  • invalid export (must be "json", "csv", or None)
  • invalid timeout (must be integer between 1 and 300)
from tv_scraper import Technicals

Technicals(export="xml")  # Raises ValueError
Technicals(timeout=0)    # Raises ValueError

docs.title: Internal Validation Helpers
docs.text:
If you are contributing to tv-scraper, use these BaseScraper methods for consistent validation:

Method Purpose self._validate_choice(val, choices) Check if a value is in an allowed set. self._validate_list(vals, choices) Batch check a list of values. self._validate_range(val, min, max) Check if a number is within bounds. self._validate_timeframe(tf) Standardized TradingView timeframe check. self._verify_symbol_exchange(exc, sym) Live existence check via Scanner API.

docs.title: Contributing to tv-scraper
docs.text: We welcome contributions from the community! This guide will help you get started with contributing to the tv-scraper project.

docs.title: Code Contributions
docs.text:
• Bug Fixes: Fix issues reported in the issue tracker
  • New Features: Implement new scraping modules or enhance existing ones
  • Performance Improvements: Optimize code for better performance
  • Documentation: Improve documentation, add examples, or fix typos

docs.title: Non-Code Contributions
docs.text:
• Bug Reports: Report bugs with detailed information
  • Feature Requests: Suggest new features or improvements
  • Documentation: Help improve or translate documentation
  • Testing: Test the library and report issues

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or higher
  • Git
  • uv (for dependency management)

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
1. Fork and Clone the Repository 

    git clone https://github.com/smitkunpara/tv-scraper.git
cd tv-scraper

  2. Set up Development Environment 

    # Install all dependencies (main + dev tools)
uv sync --extra dev

# Install in development mode
uv pip install -e .

  3. Run Tests 

    # Run all tests
uv run pytest

# Run specific test file
uv run pytest tests/test_indicators.py

  4. Build Documentation 

    # Serve docs locally for development
mkdocs serve

# Build docs for production
mkdocs build

docs.title: 📚 Versioned Docs
docs.text: This project uses MkDocs Material with mike for versioned documentation publishing.

docs.title: Local docs commands
docs.text:
# Install docs tooling
uv sync --extra dev

# Preview current docs
uv run mkdocs serve

# Preview versioned docs locally
uv run mike serve

docs.title: Release-oriented docs publishing
docs.text:
# Publish a release docs version
uv run mike deploy --update-aliases <version> <alias>

# Set the default docs version
uv run mike set-default latest

Keep the docs flow aligned with release tags, and update AGENTS.md if it still describes older docs instructions or outdated project structure.

docs.title: Writing Tests
docs.text:
• Edge Case Verification: For each new feature or scraper method, comprehensive test cases MUST be created to verify edge cases and failure modes.
  • Write tests for all new features using descriptive test names
  • Include both positive and negative (error handling) test cases
  • Mock external API calls when possible

docs.title: Test Structure
docs.text:
import pytest
from tv_scraper import CandleStreamer

class TestCandleStreamer:
    def test_get_candles_success(self):
        """Test fetching candles successfully."""
        streamer = CandleStreamer()
        result = streamer.get_candles(
            exchange="BINANCE",
            symbol="BTCUSDT",
            timeframe="1m",
            numb_candles=5
        )
        assert result["status"] == "success"
        assert "ohlcv" in result["data"]
        assert len(result["data"]["ohlcv"]) == 5

    def test_invalid_exchange(self):
        """Test error handling for invalid exchange."""
        streamer = CandleStreamer()
        result = streamer.get_candles(
            exchange="INVALID",
            symbol="BTCUSDT"
        )
        assert result["status"] == "failed"
        assert result["error"] is not None

docs.title: 📋 Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Create a Feature Branch 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

  2. Make Your Changes

  3. Write clean, well-documented code
  4. Add tests for new functionality

  5. Update documentation if needed

  6. Run Quality Checks 

    # Run tests
python -m pytest

# Build documentation
mkdocs build

  7. Commit Your Changes 

    git add .
git commit -m "Add your descriptive commit message"

  8. Push and Create Pull Request 

    git push origin feature/your-feature-name
    Then create a pull request on GitHub.

docs.title: Pull Request Guidelines
docs.text:
• Provide a clear description of the changes
  • Reference any related issues
  • Include screenshots for UI changes
  • Ensure all tests pass
  • Update documentation if needed

docs.title: Bug Report Template
docs.text:
When reporting bugs, please include:

  1. Description: Clear description of the bug
  2. Steps to Reproduce: 
    # Provide a minimal code example that reproduces the issue
  3. Expected Behavior: What should happen
  4. Actual Behavior: What actually happens
  5. Environment:
  6. Python version: 3.11
  7. OS: Windows 11
  8. Library version: 1.5.0

docs.title: Example Bug Report
docs.text:
**Bug: Technicals scraper fails with timeout**

**Steps to reproduce:**
```python
from tv_scraper import Technicals

scraper = Technicals()
result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSD",
    technical_indicators=["RSI", "MACD"]
)

Expected: Should return indicator data Actual: Times out with error Environment: Python 3.11, Windows 11 ```

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This page is the shortest path to a successful request. It covers install, the common call pattern, exports, cookies, and one invalid-input example so you can see how failures look.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install tv-scraper

Or with uv:

uv add tv-scraper

For local development:

git clone https://github.com/smitkunpara/tv-scraper.git
cd tv-scraper
uv sync --extra dev

docs.title: First Successful Call
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Technicals

scraper = Technicals()
result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    technical_indicators=["RSI", "MACD.macd"],
)

if result["status"] == "success":
    print(result["data"])
else:
    print(result["error"])

Output structure (success):

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {"RSI": 54.21, "MACD.macd": 0.15},
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "timeframe": "1d",
        "technical_indicators": ["RSI", "MACD.macd"],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

Output structure (failure):

{
    "status": "failed",
    "data": None,
    "metadata": {"exchange": "INVALID", "symbol": "AAPL", "technical_indicators": ["RSI"]},
    "warnings": [],
    "error": "Invalid value: 'INVALID'. ...",
}

Use this same pattern across the library:

  1. Create the scraper or streamer.
  2. Call a public method with keyword arguments.
  3. Check result["status"].
  4. Read result["data"] on success or result["error"] on failure.

docs.title: Common Input Pattern
docs.text:
Most symbol-based methods take exchange and symbol separately:

result = scraper.get_technicals(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

To find accepted values quickly, jump straight to:

  • Exchanges
  • Technical indicators
  • Timeframes
  • Languages

docs.title: Response Shape
docs.text:
All public methods return the same outer envelope:

{
    "status": "success" | "failed",
    "data": ...,
    "metadata": {...},
    "warnings": [str, ...],
    "error": None | "message",
}

docs.title: Exports
docs.text:
Most scrapers can export successful responses to JSON or CSV:

from tv_scraper import Technicals

scraper = Technicals(export="json")
result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    technical_indicators=["RSI"],
)

Supported export formats:

  • "json"
  • "csv"

Invalid export formats are one of the few cases that fail at construction time:

Technicals(export="xml")

docs.title: Cookie-Based Features
docs.text:
A cookie is optional for most HTTP scrapers, but required for:

  • Pine
  • authenticated streaming flows such as custom indicator access

The library will use TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE automatically when the constructor cookie argument is omitted. See Getting Cookies.

wrong input

This input shape is wrong because exchange and symbol are separate arguments.

scraper.get_technicals(exchange="NASDAQ:AAPL", symbol="AAPL")

Use this instead:

scraper.get_technicals(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

docs.text:
• Want exact request rules and response behavior: API Basics
  • Need a method-specific page: browse the scraper docs
  • Need valid values fast: Validation & Supported Values

docs.title: Getting Cookies
docs.text: Use a TradingView session cookie when a scraper hits a captcha challenge or when a feature requires authenticated access.

docs.title: When You Need A Cookie
docs.text:
• Pine always requires one
  • Ideas may need one after TradingView presents a captcha
  • CandleStreamer may need one for custom or private indicators
  • any workflow where you want the library to reuse your authenticated TradingView session

docs.title: How To Capture The Cookie
docs.text:
1. Open a TradingView page in your browser, for example https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/BTCUSD/ideas/.
  2. Open developer tools and switch to the Network tab.
  3. If a captcha appears, solve it.
  4. Refresh the page once the captcha is complete.
  5. Open the page request in the Network list.
  6. Copy the full Cookie request header value.

docs.title: Ideas
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Ideas

TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE = "paste_your_cookie_here"

scraper = Ideas(cookie=TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE)
result = scraper.get_ideas(
    exchange="CRYPTO",
    symbol="BTCUSD",
    start_page=1,
    end_page=2,
)

docs.title: Pine
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Pine

pine = Pine(cookie=TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE)
result = pine.list_saved_scripts()

docs.title: CandleStreamer With Indicators
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import CandleStreamer

streamer = CandleStreamer(cookie=TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE)
result = streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    indicators=[("STD;RSI", "37.0")],
)

docs.title: Environment Variable Option
docs.text:
If you do not want to pass the cookie to every constructor, set:

export TRADINGVIEW_COOKIE='paste_your_cookie_here'

Then create scrapers normally:

from tv_scraper import Pine

pine = Pine()

docs.title: Expiration
docs.text: Cookies are not permanent. If a request starts failing with captcha or auth-related errors again, capture a fresh cookie and retry.

docs.title: Best Way To Use This Section
docs.text:
1. Open the method page you need.
  2. Follow the direct reference link on that page.
  3. Copy the value exactly as shown here.

That keeps the scraper pages shorter and gives you one predictable place to find valid inputs.

docs.title: Releases & Versioning
docs.text: This project is set up to use versioned MkDocs builds with mike, so readers can switch between released docs when those versions are published to GitHub Pages.

docs.title: What Readers Should Expect
docs.text:
• The deployed site should offer a version switcher in the header.
  • latest should point to the newest stable branch build.
  • tagged releases such as v1.3.2 or v1.4.0 should appear as their own docs versions.

docs.title: Docs Availability
docs.text: The documentation site was introduced in v0.5.0. That means the release-aware docs story starts from v0.5.0 onward.

docs.title: Known Versions In This Repository
docs.text: Version v0.5.0 v0.5.1 v0.5.2 v1.0.0 v1.0.2 v1.0.3 v1.1.0 v1.2.0 v1.2.1 v1.3.0 v1.3.1 v1.3.2 v1.4.0 v1.4.1 v1.4.2 v1.4.3 v1.5.0

docs.title: Current Docs Target
docs.text: The current package version is 1.5.0.

docs.title: Publishing Notes
docs.text:
For maintainers:

  • branch builds should publish the moving alias such as latest
  • tag builds should publish the exact release version
  • older tags may need a backfill pass if they were created before the version switcher config was added

If repo instructions or contributor workflow docs still describe a non-versioned docs flow, update them alongside the docs changes.

docs.title: Calendar
docs.text: Calendar fetches dividend and earnings events in a date range.

docs.title: Dividends
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Calendar

scraper = Calendar()
result = scraper.get_dividends(markets=["america"])

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "dividend_ex_date_upcoming": 1763251200,
            "market": "america",
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "event_type": "dividends",
        "total": 42,
        "timestamp_from": 1762646400,
        "timestamp_to": 1763251199,
        "markets": ["america"],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Earnings
docs.text:
result = scraper.get_earnings(
    markets=["america", "uk"],
    fields=["name", "earnings_release_date", "earnings_per_share_fq"],
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "earnings_release_date": 1763164800,
            "earnings_per_share_fq": 1.42,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "event_type": "earnings",
        "total": 28,
        "timestamp_from": 1762646400,
        "timestamp_to": 1763251199,
        "markets": ["america", "uk"],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Both methods support:

Parameter Notes timestamp_from optional Unix timestamp timestamp_to optional Unix timestamp markets optional list such as ["america", "uk"] fields optional field subset for the selected event type lang optional language code

docs.title: Default Date Window
docs.text:
If you do not pass timestamps, the scraper uses a centered 7-day window:

  • timestamp_from = current midnight - 3 days
  • timestamp_to = current midnight + 3 days + 86399 seconds

wrong input

This fails if the field name is not part of the calendar field set for that method.

scraper.get_dividends(fields=["earnings_release_date"])

Notes

  • get_dividends() and get_earnings() return success with an empty data list when no events are found.
  • The fields list is validated only when it is non-empty.

docs.title: Fundamentals
docs.text: Fundamentals returns fundamental fields for one symbol through the TradingView scanner endpoint.

docs.title: All fields
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Fundamentals

scraper = Fundamentals()
result = scraper.get_fundamentals(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

docs.title: Output structure (truncated)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
        "market_cap_basic": 2950000000000,
        ...
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "fields": [...],
        "data_category": "fundamentals",
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Selected fields
docs.text:
result = scraper.get_fundamentals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    fields=["total_revenue", "net_income", "price_earnings_ttm"],
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
        "total_revenue": 416161000000,
        "net_income": 108000000000,
        "price_earnings_ttm": 29.4,
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "fields": ["total_revenue", "net_income", "price_earnings_ttm"],
        "data_category": "fundamentals",
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Parameter Notes exchange Use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol Symbol slug such as AAPL fields None or [] fetches all fields; otherwise use a list of field names from the groups below

docs.title: Field Groups
docs.text:
Group Examples Income statement total_revenue, gross_profit, net_income, EBITDA Balance sheet total_assets, total_debt, stockholders_equity Cash flow cash_f_operating_activities, free_cash_flow Margins gross_margin, operating_margin_ttm, net_margin_percent_ttm Profitability return_on_equity, return_on_assets_fq Liquidity current_ratio, quick_ratio_fq Leverage debt_to_equity, debt_to_assets Valuation market_cap_basic, price_earnings_ttm, price_sales_ttm Dividends dividends_yield, dividend_payout_ratio_ttm

wrong input

This fails because fields must be a list or None.

scraper.get_fundamentals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    fields="total_revenue",
)

Notes

  • The method verifies the live exchange:symbol before the main fetch.
  • Missing fields still appear in data with value None.

docs.title: Ideas
docs.text: Ideas fetches TradingView idea posts for a symbol and supports multiple pages plus popular or recent ordering.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Ideas

scraper = Ideas()
result = scraper.get_ideas(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    start_page=1,
    end_page=2,
    sort_by="popular",
)

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "title": "Idea title",
            "description": "Body text",
            "preview_image": [],
            "chart_url": "/chart/abc",
            "comments_count": 2,
            "views_count": 10,
            "author": "trader",
            "likes_count": 5,
            "timestamp": 1700000000,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "start_page": 1,
        "end_page": 2,
        "sort_by": "popular",
        "total": 40,
        "pages": 2,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Notes exchange use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol symbol slug such as AAPL start_page must be >= 1 end_page must be >= start_page sort_by popular or recent

wrong input

This fails because the page range is invalid.

scraper.get_ideas(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    start_page=3,
    end_page=1,
)

Notes

  • Pages are fetched concurrently.
  • Result order follows completion order, not guaranteed page order.
  • If one or more pages fail, the method currently returns status="failed" and reports collected counts in metadata instead of returning partial rows in data.

docs.title: Market Movers
docs.text: MarketMovers gives you predefined gainers, losers, most-active lists, and stock-only categories such as pre-market and after-hours movers.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import MarketMovers

scraper = MarketMovers()
result = scraper.get_market_movers(
    market="stocks-usa",
    category="gainers",
    limit=20,
    language="en",
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "close": 175.5,
            "change": 2.5,
            "change_abs": 4.28,
            "volume": 51234567,
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "market": "stocks-usa",
        "category": "gainers",
        "total": 20,
        "total_available": 7342,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Markets
docs.text:
• stocks-usa
  • stocks-uk
  • stocks-india
  • stocks-australia
  • stocks-canada
  • crypto
  • forex
  • bonds
  • futures

docs.title: Categories
docs.text:
Stock markets support:

  • gainers
  • losers
  • most-active
  • penny-stocks
  • pre-market-gainers
  • pre-market-losers
  • after-hours-gainers
  • after-hours-losers

Non-stock markets support:

  • gainers
  • losers
  • most-active

wrong input

This fails because MarketMovers expects stocks-usa, not america.

scraper.get_market_movers(market="america", category="gainers")

Notes

  • language is validated against the shared language list: Languages
  • User-provided fields are checked as a list of strings but not against a strict global field allowlist.

docs.title: Markets
docs.text: Markets returns ranked market-wide symbol lists such as the largest or most active stocks in a supported market.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Markets

scraper = Markets()
result = scraper.get_markets(
    market="america",
    sort_by="market_cap",
    sort_order="desc",
    limit=25,
)

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "close": 187.21,
            "market_cap_basic": 2800000000000.0,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "market": "america",
        "sort_by": "market_cap",
        "sort_order": "desc",
        "limit": 25,
        "total": 25,
        "total_available": 12345,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Accepted values market america, australia, canada, germany, india, uk, crypto, forex, global sort_by market_cap, volume, change, price, volatility sort_order asc, desc limit 1 to 1000 fields optional list of output columns

wrong input

This fails because Markets uses market names like america, not stocks-usa.

scraper.get_markets(market="stocks-usa")

Notes

  • This class applies stock-oriented filters internally, so non-stock markets such as crypto and forex may be more restrictive than expected.
  • If no rows come back, the method returns status="failed".

docs.title: Minds
docs.text: Minds fetches community discussion posts for a symbol with cursor-based pagination.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Minds

scraper = Minds()
result = scraper.get_minds(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL", limit=20)

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "text": "Community post text",
            "url": "/mind/1",
            "author": {
                "username": "user",
                "profile_url": "https://www.tradingview.com/u/user",
                "is_broker": False,
            },
            "created": "2024-01-01 00:00:00",
            "total_likes": 5,
            "total_comments": 2,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "limit": 20,
        "total": 20,
        "symbol_info": {...},
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Notes exchange use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol symbol slug such as AAPL limit optional final slice of the collected rows

limit is applied after pagination finishes.

wrong input

This fails because exchange and symbol must still be separate.

scraper.get_minds(exchange="NASDAQ:AAPL", symbol="AAPL")

Notes

  • The scraper stops after MAX_PAGES = 100 even if more pages exist.
  • symbol_info is included in success metadata when available.
  • If a later page fails, the method returns status="failed" and does not return partial rows in data.

docs.title: News
docs.text:
Version Note

get_news() uses the News Flow v2 API and was added in 1.4.0.

News gives you three separate entry points:

  • get_news() for flow-style filtered news
  • get_news_headlines() for symbol headlines
  • get_news_content() for full article content

docs.title: get_news(...)
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import News

scraper = News()
result = scraper.get_news(
    symbol="NASDAQ:AAPL",
    provider=["reuters"],
    market_country=["US"],
    market=["stock"],
    language="en",
    limit=10,
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "tag:provider.com,2026:newsml_123:0",
            "title": "Headline title",
            "published": 1700000000,
            "urgency": 2,
            "permission": "free",
            "relatedSymbols": [{"symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL"}],
            "storyPath": "/news/path",
            "provider": {"id": "reuters", "name": "Reuters", "logo_id": "reuters_logo"},
            "is_flash": False,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {...},
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: get_news_headlines(...)
docs.text:
result = scraper.get_news_headlines(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    provider="reuters",
    area="americas",
    sort_by="latest",
    section="all",
    language="en",
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "tag:provider.com,2026:newsml_123:0",
            "title": "Headline title",
            "shortDescription": "Short summary",
            "published": 1700000000,
            "storyPath": "/news/path",
        },
        ...,
    "metadata": {...},
    "warnings": ["get_news_headlines() is legacy. New code should prefer get_news()."],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: get_news_content(...)
docs.text:
content = scraper.get_news_content(
    story_id="tag:reuters.com,2026:newsml_example:0",
    language="en",
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": "tag:provider.com,2026:newsml_123:0",
        "title": "Headline title",
        "description": "Merged article text",
        "published": 1700000000,
        "storyPath": "/news/path",
    },
    "metadata": {...},
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: get_news(...)
docs.text:
• symbol: optional full symbol such as NASDAQ:AAPL
  • provider: News providers
  • market_country: News countries
  • sector: News sectors
  • corp_activity: News corporate activities
  • economic_category: News economic categories
  • market: News asset markets
  • language: Languages

docs.title: get_news_headlines(...)
docs.text:
• exchange: Exchanges
  • provider: News providers
  • area: News areas
  • sort_by: latest, oldest, most_urgent, least_urgent
  • section: all, esg, press_release, financial_statement
  • language: Languages

docs.title: get_news_content(...)
docs.text:
• story_id: non-empty story id string
  • language: Languages

wrong input

This is wrong for area because the input value should be the readable key, not the mapped code.

scraper.get_news_headlines(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    area="WLD",
)

Use:

scraper.get_news_headlines(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    area="world",
)

Notes

  • get_news() applies limit client-side after the fetch.
  • get_news() rejects filter combinations that would create a URL longer than 4096 characters.
  • get_news_content() requires a non-empty story_id.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Version Note

The options API has been unified around get_options(...). Use this single method for expiry-only, strike-only, or combined filtering.

Options fetches option-chain rows for one underlying symbol.

docs.title: By expiration
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Options

scraper = Options()
result = scraper.get_options(
    exchange="BSE",
    symbol="SENSEX",
    expiration=20260219,
)

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "BSE:OPTION_SYMBOL",
            "expiration": 20260219,
            "strike": 83300,
            "bid": 101.5,
            "ask": 103.0,
        },
        ...,
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "BSE",
        "symbol": "SENSEX",
        "expiration": 20260219,
        "total": 84,
        "filter_value": 20260219,
    },
    "warnings": [],

}

    exchange="BSE",
    symbol="SENSEX",
    strike=83300,

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "BSE:OPTION_SYMBOL",
            "strike": 83300,
            "bid": 101.5,
            "ask": 103.0,

docs.text:
result = scraper.get_options(
    exchange="BSE",
    symbol="SENSEX",
    expiration=20260219,
    strike=83300,
    columns=["strike", "bid", "ask", "iv"],

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "BSE:OPTION_SYMBOL",
            "strike": 83300,
            "bid": 101.5,
            "ask": 103.0,
            "iv": 18.4,
        }
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "BSE",
        "symbol": "SENSEX",
        "expiration": 20260219,
        "strike": 83300,
        "columns": ["strike", "bid", "ask", "iv"],
        "total": 1,
        "filter_value": {"expiration": 20260219, "strike": 83300},
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Notes exchange underlying exchange, such as BSE symbol underlying symbol, such as SENSEX expiration optional YYYYMMDD integer strike optional int or float columns optional list from the allowed option columns below

At least one of expiration or strike is required.

docs.title: Allowed option columns
docs.text:
ask, bid, currency, delta, expiration, gamma, iv, option-type, pricescale, rho, root, strike, theoPrice, theta, vega, bid_iv, ask_iv

wrong input

This fails because the request has no filter.

scraper.get_options(exchange="BSE", symbol="SENSEX")

Notes

  • expiration is validated as a real calendar date, not just an 8-digit number.
  • If TradingView has no option chain for the symbol, the method returns status="failed" with a chain-not-found style message.

docs.title: Pine
docs.text:
Version Note

list_saved_scripts() includes each script version in the returned data as of 1.2.1.

Pine manages authenticated Pine Script operations through the TradingView Pine facade.

docs.title: Cookie Required
docs.text:
Every public Pine method requires a TradingView cookie.

from tv_scraper import Pine

pine = Pine(cookie="paste_your_cookie_here")

If the cookie is missing, the method returns a failed response instead of making the request.

docs.title: List saved scripts
docs.text: result = pine.list_saved_scripts()

docs.title: Output structure (truncated)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "USER;abc123",
            "name": "My Script",
            "version": "5.0",
            "modified": 1700000000,
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {},
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Validate source
docs.text: result = pine.validate_script(source="//@version=6\nindicator('Demo')\nplot(close)")

docs.title: Output structure (warnings-only success)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": None,
    "metadata": {
        "source": "//@version=6\nvar x = na",
    },
    "warnings": [{"text": "Unused variable"}],
    "error": None,
}

Warning Behavior

All Pine methods now use a standardized top-level warnings field: - validate_script(), create_script(), and edit_script() return compiler warnings in the top-level warnings field.

docs.title: Create a script
docs.text:
result = pine.create_script(
    name="My Script",
    source="//@version=6\nindicator('Demo')\nplot(close)",
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": "USER;abc123",
        "name": "My Script",
    },
    "metadata": {
        "name": "My Script",
        "source": "//@version=6\nindicator('Demo')\nplot(close)",
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Edit a script
docs.text:
result = pine.edit_script(
    pine_id="USER;abc123",
    name="My Script",
    source="//@version=6\nindicator('Demo 2')\nplot(close)",
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": "USER;abc123",
        "name": "My Script",
    },
    "metadata": {
        "pine_id": "USER;abc123",
        "name": "My Script",
        "source": "//@version=6\nindicator('Demo 2')\nplot(close)",
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Fetch script content
docs.text: result = pine.get_script(pine_id="USER;abc123", version="5.0")

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "id": "USER;abc123",
        "name": "My Script",
        "title": "My Script",
        "version": "5.0",
        "last_version": 6,
        "created": 1700000000,
        "updated": 1700000500,
        "source": "//@version=6\nindicator('Demo')\nplot(close)",
        "extra": {},
    },
    "metadata": {
        "pine_id": "USER;abc123",
        "version": "5.0",
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Delete a script
docs.text: result = pine.delete_script(pine_id="USER;abc123")

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {"id": "USER;abc123"},
    "metadata": {"pine_id": "USER;abc123"},
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

wrong input

This fails because the source is empty.

pine.validate_script(source="   ")

Notes

  • create_script() and edit_script() run validate_script() internally before saving.
  • get_script() returns the raw Pine source plus metadata such as title, version, created, and updated.
  • delete_script() succeeds only when the Pine endpoint returns the expected "ok" value.

docs.title: Screener
docs.text: Screener is the flexible scan API. Use it when you want custom filters, sorting, field selection, or symbol sets.

docs.title: Basic market scan
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Screener

scraper = Screener()
result = scraper.get_screener(
    market="america",
    filters=[{"left": "close", "operation": "greater", "right": 100}],
    fields=["name", "close", "volume", "market_cap_basic"],
    sort_by="market_cap_basic",
    sort_order="desc",
    limit=25,
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "close": 187.21,
            "volume": 51234567,
            "market_cap_basic": 2800000000000.0,
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "market": "america",
        "total": 25,
        "total_available": 10342,
        ...
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Restrict to a symbol set
docs.text:
result = scraper.get_screener(
    market="america",
    symbols={"tickers": ["NASDAQ:AAPL", "NYSE:JPM"]},
    fields=["name", "close", "change"],
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "Apple Inc.",
            "close": 187.21,
            "change": 1.1,
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "market": "america",
        "symbols": {"tickers": ["NASDAQ:AAPL", "NYSE:JPM"]},
        "total": 2,
        "total_available": 2,
        ...
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Accepted values market america, australia, canada, germany, india, israel, italy, luxembourg, mexico, spain, turkey, uk, crypto, forex, cfd, futures, bonds, global sort_order asc, desc limit 1 to 10000 filters list of filter dicts filter2 advanced expression dict with operator symbols optional symbol restriction object

Supported filter operations:

greater, less, egreater, eless, equal, nequal, in_range, not_in_range, above, below, crosses, crosses_above, crosses_below, has, has_none_of

wrong input

This fails because sort_order must be asc or desc.

scraper.get_screener(market="america", sort_order="descending")

Notes

  • If no rows match, Screener returns success with data=[].
  • fields, sort_by, and symbols are passed through with minimal schema checking, so typoed field names usually become empty or None values rather than validation errors.

docs.title: Symbol Markets
docs.text: SymbolMarkets helps you find where a symbol is traded across TradingView scanners.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import SymbolMarkets

scraper = SymbolMarkets()
scraper = SymbolMarkets()
result = scraper.get_symbol_markets(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "AAPL",
            "close": 150.25,
            "exchange": "NASDAQ",
            "type": "stock",
            "currency": "USD",
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "scanner": "global",
        "total": 14,
        "total_available": 14,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

You can also pass an exchange-prefixed symbol:

result = scraper.get_symbol_markets(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "symbol": "NASDAQ:AAPL",
            "name": "AAPL",
            "close": 150.25,
            "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        },
        ...
    ],
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "scanner": "global",
        "total": 14,
        "total_available": 14,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

The scraper uses only the part after : for matching.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Accepted values symbol plain symbol or EXCHANGE:SYMBOL scanner global, america, crypto, forex, cfd limit 1 to 1000 fields optional field list

wrong input

Older examples sometimes use the wrong method name. This is wrong:

scraper.get_markets(symbol="AAPL")

Use:

scraper.get_symbol_markets(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

Notes

  • If no rows are found, the method returns status="failed".
  • Metadata keeps your original exchange and symbol inputs.

docs.title: Technicals
docs.text: Technicals fetches scanner-based indicator values for one exchange:symbol.

docs.title: Selected indicators
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import Technicals

scraper = Technicals()
result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    timeframe="1d",
    technical_indicators=["RSI", "MACD.macd"],
)

docs.title: Output structure
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "RSI": 54.36791522625663,
        "MACD.macd": -1.7690915227776713,
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "timeframe": "1d",
        "technical_indicators": ["RSI", "MACD.macd"],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: All indicators
docs.text:
result = scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    timeframe="1d"

docs.title: Output structure (truncated)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "Recommend.Other": 0.09090909090909091,
        "Recommend.All": 0.3787878787878788,
        "RSI": 54.36791522625663,
        "MACD.macd": -1.7690915227776713,
        "MACD.signal": -2.9115386038865543,
        ...

        "symbol": "AAPL",

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Notes exchange Use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol Symbol slug such as AAPL timeframe Use a supported timeframe from Timeframes technical_indicators Use supported values from Technical indicators. If None (default), all indicators are returned; otherwise only the requested names are returned.

wrong input

This fails because technical_indicators must contain supported names.

scraper.get_technicals(
    exchange="NASDAQ",
    symbol="AAPL",
    technical_indicators=["INVALID_XYZ"],
)

Notes

  • If technical_indicators is None (or omitted), the method requests the full indicator set.
  • If technical_indicators is a list, only those indicator names are returned in data.
  • Missing indicator values come back as None instead of forcing failure.
  • Daily requests use plain indicator keys; non-daily requests still return plain keys after internal suffix cleanup.

docs.title: CandleStreamer
docs.text: CandleStreamer streams OHLCV candles over TradingView WebSocket sessions. It can also attach indicator studies and provides a realtime price generator.

docs.title: Candles only
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import CandleStreamer

streamer = CandleStreamer()
result = streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    timeframe="1h",
    numb_candles=25,
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "ohlcv": [
            {
                "index": 0,
                "timestamp": 1700000000,
                "open": 185.0,
                "high": 187.0,
                "low": 184.0,
                "close": 186.0,
                "volume": 50000000,
            },
            # ... more candles (one row per candle index)
        ],
        "indicators": {},
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "BINANCE",
        "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
        "timeframe": "1h",
        "numb_candles": 25,
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Candles with indicators
docs.text:
result = streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    timeframe="15m",
    numb_candles=20,
    indicators=[("STD;RSI", "37.0")],
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "ohlcv": [
            {
                "index": 0,
                "timestamp": 1700000000,
                "open": 185.0,
                "high": 187.0,
                "low": 184.0,
                "close": 186.0,
                "volume": 50000000,
            },
            # ... more candles (one row per candle index)
        ],
        "indicators": {
            "STD;RSI": [
                {"index": 0, "timestamp": 1700000000, "0": 55.5},
                # ... more rows (one per candle index)
            ]
        },
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "BINANCE",
        "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
        "timeframe": "15m",
        "numb_candles": 20,
        "indicators": [("STD;RSI", "37.0")],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

Multiple rows

Both ohlcv and indicator arrays contain one row per candle index. If you request 5 candles with an indicator, you'll get 5 rows in both arrays. The indicator rows are keyed by study name (for example, "STD;RSI") and contain an array of values for each candle.

docs.title: Realtime prices
docs.text:
indicators = [("STD;RSI", "37.0")]
price_stream = streamer.stream_realtime_price(
    exchange="BINANCE", 
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    indicators=indicators
)

for tick in price_stream:
    price = tick["price"]
    rsi = tick["indicators"].get("STD;RSI", {}).get("0")
    print(f"Price: {price}, RSI: {rsi}")

Output structure (yielded per update):

{
    "exchange": "BINANCE",
    "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
    "price": 60125.0,
    "volume": 1234.56,
    "change": 45.0,
    "change_percent": 0.08,
    "high": 60250.0,
    "low": 59800.0,
    "open": 60010.0,
    "prev_close": 60080.0,
    "bid": 60124.5,
    "ask": 60125.5,
    "indicators": {
        "STD;RSI": {
            "index": 299, 
            "timestamp": 1700000000, 
            "0": 55.5,
            # ... other fields
        }
    },
}

Generator Behavior

Unlike get_candles(), stream_realtime_price() returns a raw generator and is not wrapped with error envelopes. It raises exceptions directly during iteration (e.g., ValidationError on invalid inputs or RuntimeError on connection failures). Wrap your iteration in a try/except block to handle exceptions gracefully.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text: Parameter Notes exchange Use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol Exchange symbol slug such as BTCUSDT timeframe Use a supported timeframe from Timeframes numb_candles Must be between 1 and 5000 indicators Optional list of (script_id, version) tuples

docs.title: Finding Indicator IDs
docs.text:
For built-in indicators:

result = CandleStreamer.get_available_indicators()

For custom indicators or private scripts, use your Pine script ID and version. A cookie may be required if your TradingView account owns the script.

wrong input

This fails because numb_candles must be at least 1.

streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    timeframe="1h",
    numb_candles=0,
)

Notes

  • The receive loop uses a packet-count cutoff, not a wall-clock timeout.
  • If no OHLCV data arrives, the method returns status="failed".
  • If you request indicators and one or more studies never resolve, the method returns status="failed" with the missing indicator IDs in the error message.

docs.title: ForecastStreamer
docs.text: ForecastStreamer captures analyst forecast fields from TradingView quote updates. It is only available for stock symbols.

docs.title: Quick Use
docs.text:
from tv_scraper import ForecastStreamer

streamer = ForecastStreamer()
result = streamer.get_forecast(exchange="NASDAQ", symbol="AAPL")

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "revenue_currency": "USD",
        "previous_close_price": 171.2,
        "average_price_target": 195.0,
        "highest_price_target": 220.0,
        "lowest_price_target": 175.0,
        "median_price_target": 193.0,
        "yearly_eps_data": [
            {
                "Actual": 2.3025,
                "Estimate": 2.248051,
                "FiscalPeriod": "2017",
                "IsReported": True,
                "Type": 22,
            },
            # ... more yearly EPS rows
        ],
        "quarterly_eps_data": [
            {
                "Actual": 1.4,
                "Estimate": 0.986396,
                "FiscalPeriod": "2021-Q2",
                "IsReported": True,
                "Type": 22,
            },
            # ... more quarterly EPS rows
        ],
        "yearly_revenue_data": [
            {
                "Actual": 229234000000,
                "Estimate": 227461939394,
                "FiscalPeriod": "2017",
                "IsReported": True,
                "Type": 22,
            },
            # ... more yearly revenue rows
        ],
        "quarterly_revenue_data": [
            {
                "Actual": 89584000000,
                "Estimate": 77088700724,
                "FiscalPeriod": "2021-Q2",
                "IsReported": True,
                "Type": 22,
            },
            # ... more quarterly revenue rows
        ],
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "NASDAQ",
        "symbol": "AAPL",
        "available_output_keys": [
            "average_price_target",
            "highest_price_target",
            "lowest_price_target",
            "median_price_target",
            "previous_close_price",
            "quarterly_eps_data",
            "quarterly_revenue_data",
            "revenue_currency",
            "yearly_eps_data",
            "yearly_revenue_data",
        ],
    },
    "warnings": [],
    "error": None,
}

Partial result behavior:

  • if one or more required keys are still missing after capture, the method returns status="failed"
  • data is still included
  • missing keys are listed in error

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
Parameter Notes exchange Use a supported exchange from Exchanges symbol Stock symbol slug such as AAPL

The method verifies the symbol first and then checks the symbol type. If the instrument is not a stock, the call fails.

docs.title: Output Keys
docs.text:
The data payload always uses these keys:

  • revenue_currency
  • previous_close_price
  • average_price_target
  • highest_price_target
  • lowest_price_target
  • median_price_target
  • yearly_eps_data
  • quarterly_eps_data
  • yearly_revenue_data
  • quarterly_revenue_data

Data Structure

The yearly_eps_data, quarterly_eps_data, yearly_revenue_data, and quarterly_revenue_data fields are lists of row objects. Each row includes Actual, Estimate, FiscalPeriod, IsReported, and Type. FiscalPeriod is yearly (for example, "2017") or quarterly (for example, "2021-Q2") depending on the field.

wrong input

This fails because forecast data is stock-only.

streamer.get_forecast(exchange="BINANCE", symbol="BTCUSDT")

Notes

  • The capture loop stops once all required keys are found or the packet limit is reached.
  • Export runs only on full success.

docs.title: Streamer (Legacy Wrapper)
docs.text:
Deprecated

Streamer is deprecated. New code should use CandleStreamer and ForecastStreamer directly.

Version Note

The deprecation is documented in the current changelog for the 1.4.0 line of development.

docs.title: What It Still Does
docs.text:
Streamer keeps the older combined interface:

  • get_candles(...)
  • get_forecast(...)
  • stream_realtime_price(...)
  • get_available_indicators()

Internally:

  • candle-related calls delegate to CandleStreamer
  • forecast calls delegate to ForecastStreamer

docs.title: Replacement Mapping
docs.text:
Legacy call Preferred replacement Streamer.get_candles(...) CandleStreamer.get_candles(...) Streamer.stream_realtime_price(...) CandleStreamer.stream_realtime_price(...) Streamer.get_available_indicators() CandleStreamer.get_available_indicators() Streamer.get_forecast(...) ForecastStreamer.get_forecast(...)

Legacy Example

from tv_scraper import Streamer

streamer = Streamer()
result = streamer.get_candles(
    exchange="BINANCE",
    symbol="BTCUSDT",
    timeframe="1h",
    numb_candles=10,
)

Output structure:

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": {
        "ohlcv": [
            {
                "index": 0,
                "timestamp": 1700000000,
                "open": 185.0,
                "high": 187.0,
                "low": 184.0,
                "close": 186.0,
                "volume": 50000000,
            },
            # ... more candles (one row per candle index)
        ],
        "indicators": {},
    },
    "metadata": {
        "exchange": "BINANCE",
        "symbol": "BTCUSDT",
        "timeframe": "1h",
        "numb_candles": 10,
    },
    "warnings": [
        "The 'Streamer' class is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. "
        "Use 'CandleStreamer' or 'ForecastStreamer' instead."
    ],
    "error": None,
}

docs.title: Recommendation
docs.text: Keep this page only for migration or backward compatibility. For new examples, prefer the dedicated streamer classes.
